name: 01-hn-top-stories
instruction: Extract the top 5 story titles and their point counts. Return JSON: { "stories": [{ "title": string, "points": number }] }

name: 02-wikipedia-intro
instruction: Extract the first paragraph of the article (the lede, at least 100 chars). Return JSON: { "intro": string }
successCheckSrc: r=>{const intro=findStr(r.data,"web scraping")||findValue(r.data,v=>typeof v==="string"&&v.length>100);return intro?{success:true,reason:`${String(intro).length} chars`}:{success:false,reason:"no intro"}}

name: 03-npm-react-version
instruction: Extract the current version and weekly downloads. Return JSON: { "version": string, "weeklyDownloads": string | number }

name: 04-wikipedia-linus-nationality
instruction: From the infobox, extract Linus Torvalds' nationality. Return JSON: { "nationality": string }

name: 05-github-typescript-stats
instruction: Extract star count, primary language, and latest release. Return JSON: { "stars": string | number, "language": string, "latestRelease": string | null }

name: 06-coingecko-btc-price
instruction: Extract the current Bitcoin price in USD (as a number). Return JSON: { "priceUSD": number }

name: 07-mdn-html-elements
instruction: Extract the names of any 5 HTML elements (like "div", "span"). Return JSON: { "elements": string[] }

name: 08-stackoverflow-git-undo
instruction: Extract the question title, vote count, and first sentence of the accepted answer. Return JSON: { "title": string, "votes": number, "acceptedAnswer": string }
successCheckSrc: r=>{const t=findStr(r.data,"undo")||findStr(r.data,"commit");const v=findValue(r.data,v2=>typeof v2==="number"&&v2>100);return t&&v!==void 0?{success:true,reason:`votes=${v}`}:{success:false,reason:"missing title or votes"}}

name: 09-lobsters-stories
instruction: Extract the top 5 story titles and scores. Return JSON: { "stories": [{ "title": string, "score": number }] }

name: 10-books-toscrape
instruction: Extract the first 5 book titles and prices. Return JSON: { "books": [{ "title": string, "price": string }] }

name: 11-github-react-stats
instruction: Extract star count and primary language. Return JSON: { "stars": string | number, "language": string }

name: 12-wikipedia-python
instruction: Extract the programming paradigms listed in the infobox (e.g. "multi-paradigm", "object-oriented"). Return JSON: { "paradigms": string }

name: 13-scrapethissite-countries
instruction: Extract the first 5 country names and their capitals. Return JSON: { "countries": [{ "name": string, "capital": string }] }

name: 14-npm-express-info
instruction: Extract the package version and description. Return JSON: { "version": string, "description": string }
successCheckSrc: r=>{const v=findValue(r.data,v2=>typeof v2==="string"&&/^\d+\.\d+/.test(v2));const d=findValue(r.data,v2=>typeof v2==="string"&&v2.length>10&&v2.length<200);return v&&d?{success:true,reason:`v${v}`}:{success:false,reason:"missing version or desc"}}

name: 15-the-internet-tables
instruction: Extract the data from the first table (Table 1). Return JSON: { "rows": [{ "lastName": string, "firstName": string, "email": string }] } for the first 4 rows.

name: 16-hn-comments
instruction: Click on the "comments" link of the first story. After the comments page loads, extract the story title. Return JSON: { "storyTitle": string }

name: 17-github-issues
instruction: Extract the titles of the first 5 open issues. Return JSON: { "issues": [{ "title": string }] }

name: 18-github-releases
instruction: Extract the title and date of the 3 most recent releases. Return JSON: { "releases": [{ "title": string, "date": string }] }

name: 19-books-page2
instruction: Click the "next" button to go to page 2, then extract the first 3 book titles from page 2. Return JSON: { "books": [{ "title": string }] }

name: 20-mdn-array-methods
instruction: Extract 5 Array instance method names from this page (like "map", "filter", "reduce"). Return JSON: { "methods": string[] }

name: 21-quotes-tag-page
instruction: Click on the "love" tag link to navigate to the love quotes page. Then extract the first 3 quotes and their authors. Return JSON: { "quotes": [{ "text": string, "author": string }] }

name: 22-books-science-category
instruction: Click on the "Science" category link in the sidebar, then extract the first 3 book titles and prices from the Science category page. Return JSON: { "books": [{ "title": string, "price": string }] }

name: 23-wikipedia-search-openai
instruction: Type "OpenAI" into the search field and press Enter to submit. Extract the first 3 result titles. Return JSON: { "results": [{ "title": string }] }

name: 24-wikipedia-search-rust
instruction: Type "Rust programming language" into the search field and press Enter to submit. Extract the first 3 result titles. Return JSON: { "results": [{ "title": string }] }

name: 25-npm-search-express
instruction: Extract the names and descriptions of the first 3 packages. Return JSON: { "packages": [{ "name": string, "description": string }] }

name: 26-quotes-pagination
instruction: Click the "Next" button to go to page 2. Extract the first 3 quotes and authors from page 2. Return JSON: { "quotes": [{ "text": string, "author": string }] }

name: 27-scrapethissite-hockey
instruction: This page has a table of hockey teams. Extract the first 5 team names and their wins. Return JSON: { "teams": [{ "name": string, "wins": number }] }

name: 28-weather-gov-sf
instruction: Extract the current temperature and conditions for San Francisco. Return JSON: { "temperature": string, "conditions": string }

name: 29-httpbin-pizza
instruction: Fill: customer name "Test User", phone "555-1234", email "test@example.com", comments "Ring the bell". Click Submit. Extract the customer name from the response. Return JSON: { "submittedName": string }

name: 30-demoqa-textbox
instruction: Fill: Full Name "Alice Smith", Email "alice@example.com", Current Address "123 Main St", Permanent Address "456 Oak Ave". Click Submit. Extract the displayed output. Return JSON: { "name": string, "email": string }

name: 31-quotes-login
instruction: Fill username "admin" and password "admin", click Login. Return JSON: { "loggedIn": true } if successful.

name: 32-the-internet-login
instruction: Fill username "tomsmith" and password "SuperSecretPassword!", click Login. Return JSON: { "loggedIn": true } if you see the secure area page.

name: 33-httpbin-anything
instruction: Fill the form with customer "Bob Jones", size "Large", and topping "Bacon". Click Submit. Extract the customer name. Return JSON: { "customer": string }

name: 34-the-internet-dropdown
instruction: Select "Option 2" from the dropdown menu. Return JSON: { "selected": string } with the selected value.

name: 35-the-internet-checkboxes
instruction: Check the first checkbox (if unchecked) and uncheck the second (if checked). Then report the state of both. Return JSON: { "checkbox1": boolean, "checkbox2": boolean }

name: 36-wikipedia-search-submit-extract
instruction: Type "machine learning" into the search field and press Enter to submit. Extract the first 3 result titles. Return JSON: { "results": [{ "title": string }] }

name: 37-quotes-login-then-scrape
instruction: Log in with username "admin" and password "admin". After login, extract the first 3 quotes and their authors from the homepage. Return JSON: { "quotes": [{ "text": string, "author": string }] }

name: 38-github-nextjs-stats
instruction: Extract the star count, primary language, and description. Return JSON: { "stars": string | number, "language": string, "description": string }

name: 39-npm-zod-info
instruction: Extract the version number and description. Return JSON: { "version": string, "description": string }

name: 40-wikipedia-js-intro
instruction: Extract the first paragraph of the article body. Return JSON: { "intro": string }

name: 41-example-com
instruction: Extract the main heading text and the first paragraph text. Return JSON: { "heading": string, "paragraph": string }
successCheckSrc: r=>{const h=findStr(r.data,"example domain");return h?{success:true,reason:"example domain"}:{success:false,reason:"no heading"}}

name: 42-jsonplaceholder-users
instruction: This page shows raw JSON. Extract the names and emails of the first 3 users. Return JSON: { "users": [{ "name": string, "email": string }] }

name: 44-turnstile-login
instruction: Fill username "testuser" and password "testpass", wait for Turnstile to validate, then click Sign in. Return JSON: { "submitted": true } if successful.

name: 45-wikipedia-rust-intro
instruction: Extract the first paragraph of the article. Return JSON: { "intro": string }

name: 46-mdn-css-display
instruction: Extract the formal syntax values for the CSS display property (e.g. "block", "inline", "flex", "grid", "none"). Return JSON: { "values": string[] }

name: 47-quotes-all-tags
instruction: Extract the top 10 tags shown in the sidebar tag cloud. Return JSON: { "tags": string[] }

name: 48-github-deno-releases
instruction: Extract the latest release version tag (e.g. "v2.3.1") and its title. Return JSON: { "version": string, "title": string }

name: 49-books-toscrape-details
instruction: Extract the book title, price, availability, and number of reviews. Return JSON: { "title": string, "price": string, "availability": string, "reviews": number }
successCheckSrc: r=>{const t=findStr(r.data,"light in the attic");const p=findValue(r.data,v=>typeof v==="string"&&/[£$\d]/.test(v));return t&&p?{success:true,reason:String(t).slice(0,30)}:{success:false,reason:"missing data"}}

name: 50-quotes-toscrape-specific
instruction: Extract all quotes by Albert Einstein on this page (there should be at least 1). Return JSON: { "quotes": [{ "text": string }] }

name: 51-books-category-extract
instruction: Click on the "Travel" category in the sidebar, then extract all book titles listed. Return JSON: { "books": [{ "title": string }] }

name: 52-books-mystery-category
instruction: Navigate to the Mystery category and extract the first 5 book titles and prices. Return JSON: { "books": [{ "title": string, "price": string }] }

name: 53-books-click-and-detail
instruction: Click on the first book shown on the homepage, then extract its full title, price, UPC, and stock availability. Return JSON: { "title": string, "price": string, "upc": string, "stock": string }

name: 54-books-paginate-count
instruction: Count the number of books displayed on this catalogue page. Return JSON: { "count": number }
successCheckSrc: r=>{const n=findValue(r.data,v=>typeof v==="number"&&v>0);return n?{success:true,reason:`${n} books`}:{success:false,reason:"no count"}}

name: 55-quotes-next-page
instruction: Click the Next page button and extract the first 3 quote texts from page 2. Return JSON: { "quotes": [{ "text": string }] }

name: 56-quotes-author-page
instruction: Click on the first author's name link (not the tag), then extract the author's birth date and place from the author detail page. Return JSON: { "bornDate": string, "bornPlace": string }
successCheckSrc: r=>{const d=findValue(r.data,v=>typeof v==="string"&&v.length>3);return d?{success:true,reason:"got data"}:{success:false,reason:"no data"}}

name: 57-quotes-tag-filter
instruction: Click on the "love" tag, then return the number of quotes shown on the filtered page. Return JSON: { "count": number }

name: 58-wikipedia-follow-link
instruction: Click on the "ECMAScript" link in the article and extract the first paragraph of the resulting page. Return JSON: { "intro": string }

name: 59-wikipedia-infobox-data
instruction: Extract from the infobox on the right side: the designer name, the date of first appearance, and the current stable release version. Return JSON: { "designer": string, "firstAppeared": string, "stableRelease": string }

name: 60-wikipedia-references-count
instruction: Scroll to the References section and count how many references are listed. Return JSON: { "count": number }
successCheckSrc: r=>{const n=findValue(r.data,v=>typeof v==="number"&&v>5);return n?{success:true,reason:`${n} refs`}:{success:false,reason:"too few"}}

name: 61-hn-comments-navigate
instruction: Click on the comments link of the top story and extract the title of that story and the number of comments. Return JSON: { "title": string, "commentCount": number }
successCheckSrc: r=>{const t=findValue(r.data,v=>typeof v==="string"&&v.length>3);const c=findValue(r.data,v=>typeof v==="number"&&v>=0);return t&&c!==void 0?{success:true,reason:"got both"}:{success:false,reason:"missing"}}

name: 62-hn-newest-stories
instruction: Click the "new" link in the top navigation, then extract the top 3 story titles. Return JSON: { "stories": [{ "title": string }] }

name: 63-hn-user-profile
instruction: Extract the user's karma score and account creation date. Return JSON: { "karma": number, "createdDate": string }

name: 64-github-repo-readme-header
instruction: Extract the repository description (the tagline at the top) and the programming language used. Return JSON: { "description": string, "language": string }

name: 65-github-navigate-to-issues
instruction: Click on the Issues tab and extract the titles of the top 3 open issues. Return JSON: { "issues": [{ "title": string }] }

name: 66-mdn-javascript-array-methods
instruction: Extract the first 5 method names from the instance methods section. Return JSON: { "methods": [string] }

name: 67-mdn-fetch-parameters
instruction: Extract the parameters listed for the fetch() function. Return JSON: { "parameters": [string] }

name: 68-npm-package-version
instruction: Extract the current version number and weekly download count. Return JSON: { "version": string, "weeklyDownloads": string }

name: 69-npm-lodash-dependencies
instruction: Extract the package maintainer and the license type. Return JSON: { "maintainer": string, "license": string }

name: 71-httpbin-html-table
instruction: Extract the author and title from the page. Return JSON: { "author": string, "title": string }

name: 72-jsonplaceholder-nav-post
instruction: Navigate to /posts/1 and extract the title and body. Return JSON: { "title": string, "body": string }

name: 73-w3schools-css-colors
instruction: Extract the first 5 CSS color names and their hex codes from the reference table. Return JSON: { "colors": [{ "name": string, "hex": string }] }

name: 74-scrapethissite-countries
instruction: Extract the first 5 country names and their capitals from the list. Return JSON: { "countries": [{ "name": string, "capital": string }] }

name: 75-scrapethissite-hockey-table
instruction: Extract the first 5 team names and their years from the hockey teams table. Return JSON: { "teams": [{ "name": string, "year": number }] }

name: 76-the-internet-dropdown
instruction: Select "Option 2" from the dropdown and then return the currently selected option text. Return JSON: { "selected": string }

name: 77-the-internet-checkboxes
instruction: Check the first checkbox and uncheck the second, then return the final checked state of both. Return JSON: { "first": boolean, "second": boolean }

name: 78-the-internet-dynamic-content
instruction: Extract the text content of the first 3 paragraphs on the page. Return JSON: { "paragraphs": [string] }

name: 79-the-internet-add-remove
instruction: Click the "Add Element" button 3 times, then return the number of "Delete" buttons visible. Return JSON: { "count": number }

name: 80-the-internet-iframe
instruction: Extract the heading text from the page. Return JSON: { "heading": string }

name: 81-books-home-to-category-to-book
instruction: Click the Travel category, then click the first book, then extract its UPC code. Return JSON: { "upc": string }

name: 82-wikipedia-search-result
instruction: Type "Rust (programming language)" into the Wikipedia search box and press Enter to submit, click the top result, and extract the year it was designed. Return JSON: { "designedYear": number }

name: 83-quotes-login-and-scrape
instruction: Login with username "user" and password "user", then go to the homepage and extract the first 2 quote texts. Return JSON: { "quotes": [{ "text": string }] }

name: 84-books-sort-or-filter
instruction: Extract the total number of fiction books shown and the lowest-priced book title. Return JSON: { "count": number, "cheapestTitle": string }
successCheckSrc: r=>{const n=findValue(r.data,v=>typeof v==="number"&&v>0);const t=findValue(r.data,v=>typeof v==="string"&&v.length>2);return n&&t?{success:true,reason:"got both"}:{success:false,reason:"missing"}}

name: 85-scrapethissite-paginate
instruction: Click on the "Hockey Teams" link, then extract the first 3 team names. Return JSON: { "teams": [string] }

name: 86-hn-filter-by-points
instruction: Extract the titles of stories that have more than 100 points. Return JSON: { "stories": [{ "title": string, "points": number }] }

name: 87-books-price-under
instruction: Extract the titles and prices of the first 3 books with a price under £20. Return JSON: { "books": [{ "title": string, "price": string }] }

name: 88-wikipedia-list-items
instruction: Extract the first 10 programming language names from the list. Return JSON: { "languages": [string] }

name: 89-wikipedia-table-data
instruction: Extract the names and heights (in meters) of the top 3 tallest buildings from the table. Return JSON: { "buildings": [{ "name": string, "heightM": number }] }

name: 90-wikipedia-category-listing
instruction: Extract the first 5 framework names listed on the category page. Return JSON: { "frameworks": [string] }

name: 91-example-domain-extract
instruction: Extract the h1 text and the URL in the "More information..." link. Return JSON: { "heading": string, "linkUrl": string }

name: 92-quotes-scroll-mode
instruction: Extract the first 5 quote texts shown on this infinite-scroll page. Return JSON: { "quotes": [string] }

name: 93-quotes-js-rendered
instruction: Extract the first 3 quote authors from the JS-rendered page. Return JSON: { "authors": [string] }

name: 94-hn-ask-stories
instruction: Extract the titles and point counts of the top 3 Ask HN stories. Return JSON: { "stories": [{ "title": string, "points": number }] }

name: 95-hn-show-stories
instruction: Extract the top 3 Show HN story titles. Return JSON: { "stories": [{ "title": string }] }

name: 96-github-trending-like
instruction: Extract the names of the first 3 trending repositories shown. Return JSON: { "repos": [string] }

name: 97-mdn-html-elements
instruction: Extract the first 5 HTML element names listed in the reference. Return JSON: { "elements": [string] }

name: 98-books-add-to-cart-not
instruction: Find the first book whose title starts with "The" and return its title and price. Return JSON: { "title": string, "price": string }
successCheckSrc: r=>{const s=findValue(r.data,v=>typeof v==="string"&&/^the\s/i.test(v));return s?{success:true,reason:'found "The..."'}:{success:false,reason:"no The title"}}

name: 99-jsonplaceholder-todos
instruction: Extract the titles of the first 3 todos from the JSON response displayed. Return JSON: { "todos": [string] }

name: 100-scrapethissite-ajax
instruction: Click the 2015 link/button and extract the first 3 film titles and their Oscars count. Return JSON: { "films": [{ "title": string, "oscars": number }] }

name: trivial-example-h1
instruction: Extract the H1 heading text from the page. Return JSON: { "heading": string }
successCheckSrc: (r) => { const s = findStr(r.data, 'example'); return s ? { success: true, reason: s.slice(0, 40) } : { success: false, reason: 'no example' }; }

name: trivial-jsonplaceholder-user
instruction: Extract the user's name from the JSON response. Return JSON: { "name": string }
successCheckSrc: (r) => { const s = findStr(r.data, 'leanne') || findStr(r.data, 'graham'); return s ? { success: true, reason: s.slice(0, 40) } : { success: false, reason: 'no name' }; }
